intro: Optimal stash setup reduces time spent sorting and lets you find items instantly. Premium tabs and specific tab types pay for themselves in time saved.

tabs.name: Currency
tabs.what_to_store: All currency orbs — Chaos, Divine, Exalted, Orbs of Alteration, Transmutation, Augmentation, Annulment, etc.
tabs.naming_tip: Name it 'Currency' — auto-identified by trade sites
tabs.notes: The Currency stash tab auto-stacks all currency. Essential for every character. Trade sites index it automatically if set to public.

tabs.name: Map
tabs.what_to_store: All maps T1–T16, unique maps, blighted maps, boss fragments
tabs.naming_tip: Name it 'Maps' — matches trade site conventions
tabs.notes: The Map stash tab organises maps by tier automatically. Essential for endgame. Shows completion indicators per map.

tabs.name: Essences
tabs.what_to_store: All essences — Weeping through Deafening, plus Delirium essences (Horror/Hysteria/Insanity/Delirium)
tabs.naming_tip: Name it 'Essence' for trade site indexing
tabs.notes: One or two quad tabs work well. Sort essences by tier — Deafening at top, Weeping at bottom. Delirium essences are bulk trade items.

tabs.name: Div Cards
tabs.what_to_store: All divination cards — complete sets for turning in, partial sets for bulk trade
tabs.notes: The Divination stash tab auto-stacks cards. Separate high-value cards (The Doctor, Brother's Gift) into their own section if desired.

tabs.name: Fragments
tabs.what_to_store: Scarabs, offerings, breach splinters, legion emblems, invitations, vaal fragments, Maven's Writ
tabs.notes: Fragment tab auto-organises by type. Keeps your main stash clear of the small stackable items that accumulate fastest.

tabs.name: Gems
tabs.what_to_store: Gems being levelled, high-value Awakened gems, 20/20 gems ready to sell, corrupted gems
tabs.naming_tip: Name it 'Gems' — split into 'Gems: Level' and 'Gems: Sell' if using two tabs
tabs.notes: Keep gems being levelled in off-hand slots on every character. Move to stash when 20+. Awakened gems and 20/20 are bulk trade items.

tabs.name: Flasks
tabs.what_to_store: Rolled magic flasks, unique flasks, silver/quicksilver for levelling alts
tabs.notes: Keep a set of rolled flasks per build archetype. Label flask tabs by build if using multiple characters.

tabs.name: Crafting Bases
tabs.what_to_store: High-ilvl bases (ilvl 82–86) for crafting — helmets, chests, boots, gloves, weapons, jewellery
tabs.notes: A quad tab gives room to keep bases by slot. Only keep ilvl 82+ for meaningful crafting. Vendor anything below ilvl 80 unless it has a special implicit.

tabs.name: Unique Items
tabs.what_to_store: Useful unique equipment and jewels — keep per build, vendor/sell low-value duplicates
tabs.naming_tip: Name it 'Uniques' or split by category ('Uniq: Equip', 'Uniq: Jewel')
tabs.notes: Only keep uniques you will use. Vendor useless uniques (Orb of Chance recipe: 1 unique = 1 Chaos for full sets). Check poe.ninja for values before vendoring.

tabs.name: Dump Tab
tabs.what_to_store: Items from a mapping session before sorting — rares, uniques, chaos recipe pieces
tabs.naming_tip: Name it 'DUMP' in all caps — easy to identify after a session
tabs.notes: The quad dump tab is where items land first. Sort it after each session. Keep chaos recipe rares (ilvl 60–74 unidentified) in a separate 'Chaos' quad tab.

tabs.name: Chaos Recipe
tabs.what_to_store: Unidentified rare items ilvl 60–74 for Chaos recipe, and ilvl 75+ for Regal recipe
tabs.naming_tip: Name it 'Chaos' and make it public — trade sites can index unidentified rares for recipe tracking tools
tabs.notes: Keep one quad tab per recipe tier if you run chaos recipe actively. Helmet/ring slots fill fastest. Boots/gloves slowest.

tabs.name: Heist
tabs.what_to_store: Heist contracts, blueprints, trinkets, Rogue's Markers
tabs.notes: Keep contracts by rogue level or job type if doing targeted Heist. Bulk-sell low-level contracts to rogues for markers if not actively running Heist.

tabs.name: Expedition
tabs.what_to_store: Expedition logbooks — group by faction if doing targeted logbook farming
tabs.notes: Logbooks are bulky. Stack by faction: Kalguuran (currency), Druids (life/mana), Black Scythe (physical), Order (jewellery). Sell unwanted factions in bulk.

principles: Set high-value tabs (Currency, Fragments, Div Cards) to public for trade site indexing
A quad tab holds 4× the items of a normal tab — use them for bulk storage
Name tabs consistently: trade sites auto-detect tabs named 'Currency', 'Maps', 'Essence'
Vendor chaos recipe rares immediately after a session — don't let them pile up
The Fragment tab pays for itself — scarabs, emblems, and splinters add up fast

tab_types.description: Organises maps by tier with completion indicators.
4× capacity of a normal tab. Best for bulk storage.
Can be renamed, coloured, and set public for trade indexing.
